findings.description: Only one rule (canonical_content_block_pattern) was extracted for this blob, covering u_0004 alone. Units u_0001 (focal point limiting), u_0002 (show-don't-tell imagery rule), and u_0003 (color-block background alternation + governance clause) contain distinct, independently testable design rules that were dropped entirely rather than clustered or split into their own rules.

payload.note: Extract separate rules for focal-point limiting, show-don't-tell imagery, and section background color-block alternation, in addition to the retained canonical_content_block_pattern rule.

findings.description: The explicit [GENERAL] governance clause requiring section designers to declare background color in component metadata (to prevent adjacent duplicate backgrounds) has no corresponding rule at all. This is a compliance/process statement with a named actor (section designers) and a concrete requirement, and needs its own governance-tagged rule.

payload.statement: Section designers must declare their section's background color in component metadata so assemblers can sequence sections without two identical adjacent backgrounds.

findings.description: The bolded directive 'Limit focal points per section — use contrast to direct attention to the single most important element' is a distinct hard layout rule that was not extracted as its own rule; it is currently unrepresented.

payload.statement: Limit focal points per section; use contrast to direct attention to the single most important element.

findings.description: The 'Show, don't tell' guidance to break up text walls with imagery and icons is a discrete layout/content rule that has no corresponding extracted rule.

payload.statement: Break up text walls with imagery and icons ('show, don't tell').

findings.description: The core layout rule about alternating White/Light Blue/Golden Sand/gradient section backgrounds so adjacent sections don't visually merge is separate from the governance metadata clause and is missing as its own layout rule.

payload.statement: Alternate White / Light Blue / Golden Sand / gradient section backgrounds to delineate topics so adjacent assembled sections don't share the same background color.

findings.description: Source states the patient brochure content block pattern is the 'reference template for visuals, messaging, and overall tone of ALL LISRAYA materials' — broader than a pure 'layout' class scope; the rule's class may need to also flag messaging/tone scope or a cross-reference relation rather than being purely layout-scoped.

payload.scope_note: Applies as reference template for visuals, messaging, and tone across ALL Lisraya materials, not layout alone.
